content.problemStatement: Write a C program that calculates and prints the sum of all even numbers from 1 to a given positive integer n. Use a while loop to iterate through the numbers.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of all even numbers from 2 to N inclusive.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Even numbers up to 6 are 2,4,6; their sum is 12.
Even numbers up to 9 are 2,4,6,8; their sum is 20.
Even numbers up to 8 are 2,4,6,8; their sum is 20.

solution.approach: The even numbers form an arithmetic progression: 2,4,6,...,2·⌊N/2⌋. The number of terms is k = ⌊N/2⌋ and the sum of an AP is k·(first+last)/2 = k·(2+2k)/2 = k·(k+1). Thus for each N we compute k = N/2 (integer division) and answer = k*(k+1). This is O(1) per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total
